title: [InstCombine] Simplify nonnull phi nodes
summary: The developers generalized the optimization by extending `simplifyNonNullOperand` to handle PHI nodes, enabling the transformation to propagate through control flow. Originally, the function only processed select instructions to fold null-based GEPs into poison. The patch adds PHI support by recursively simplifying each incoming value, using the recursion limit to bound cost. This allows patterns where a PHI's operand is a select (or other null-producing instruction) to be optimized, as seen in the loop test case. The key insight is to treat PHIs as another source of null propagation, applying the same dereferenceability logic iteratively. For developers: when generalizing a local pattern, identify the core condition (here, null folding) and extend it to upstream IR constructs (PHIs) that can feed the pattern, using existing recursion safeguards.
